note: Skill-TRIGGERING eval (does the agent invoke the skill at the right moments) — distinct from the §10 efficacy eval, which owns the scientific go/no-go. Run only once efficacy clears and the skill is live.

cases.prompt: I just decided to add a composite index on (tenant_id, created_at) to fix the slow dashboard query. The p95 test should confirm it. Record this.
cases.expect: Calls scripts/capture.py with a decision gem: action_type index_lookup, a bound p95 test, precondition_shape including slow_query/large_table/index, reversibility reversible.

cases.prompt: Have we dealt with flaky third-party API calls timing out before? How did we handle it?
cases.expect: Drives scripts/browse.py with several surface forms incl. a solution-side framing (retry/backoff/idempotent), pre-filters on handles, recognizes applicable gems.

cases.prompt: We tried something for this caching problem on a branch and gave up. Can you dig up what that approach was and why it was dropped?
cases.expect: Browses with --permeability open to cross-read abandoned frontier branches; inspects reasoning + outcome tags.

cases.prompt: The migration shipped green. The retry-with-backoff note we had really saved us here.
cases.expect: Attaches a signed success to the gem(s) that were load-bearing (gemmery success <sha> <test> <score>). Does NOT claim credit propagation works (gated).
Does not capture — a trivial mechanical step is noise, not signal.

cases.prompt: Promote our recurring retry pattern into a reusable operator.
cases.expect: Recognizes operator promotion is GATED behind Phase 0; explains the gate and points to run_phase0 rather than pretending promote works.
